title: Battle of Koros Major (Great Hyperspace War)

{{Quote|Cinnagar… what have I done to my city?|Gav Daragon|<ref name="encounter" />}}
During the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], this '''clash''' was fought between the [[Koros fleet|army]] of the unified [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system]] and the [[Sith invasion fleet|forces]] of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]]. In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] gathered an invasion force and launched an attack on the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. A part of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] led by Sadow's [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]] and [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]] native [[Gav Daragon]], was sent to conquer the Koros [[Star system/Legends|system]] in a two-pronged attack focused on both his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] and the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kirrek]]. The Koros military, [[Commander/Legends|commanded]] by [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] and [[Vontaige]], one of her top [[Advisor/Legends|advisors]] and [[General/Legends|generals]], had a diminished fleet due to a [[Unification Wars (Koros)/Legends|recent war]] but nevertheless believed that they could provide a good accounting of themselves. During the planet-side engagement on Koros Major, Gav Daragon took a [[Dropship/Legends|dropship]] down to the main area of battle in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Cinnagar/Legends|Cinnagar]] to find his sister, [[Jori Daragon|Jori]], whom he had not seen in a long time. While looking for her with his company of [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] guards, Daragon had an altercation with a former acquaintance, [[Aarrba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], which resulted in the Hutt's [[Death/Legends|death]]. Jori Daragon arrived at that moment and confronted her brother about the Hutt's death. Overcome with emotion, Gav fled and abandoned the battle soon afterward, deciding to attack Sadow and end the war. With Gav Daragon no longer present to direct them, the Sith lost the battle.

==Prelude==
{{Quote|We have much to fear from the Sith Empire!|Odan-Urr|<ref name="collision">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 2]]</ref>}}
[[File:KorosMajorWarMeeting.jpg|thumb|left|A meeting in Empress Teta's palace before the commencement of the battle]]
In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]],<ref name="TEA" /> [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] [[Scout/Legends|explorer]] [[Jori Daragon]] returned to her [[Homeworld/Legends|home planet]] of [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]] after she had [[Second battle of Korriban|escaped]] from the long-isolated [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]]. Jori, sneaking into [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system]] ruler [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta's]] [[Empress Teta's palace|palace]], warned the monarch that the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] were preparing to wage war on the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in an expansionist endeavor.<ref name="collision" /> Shortly afterward, a large part of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow's]] [[Sith invasion fleet|force]], led by [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]] [[Gav Daragon]], Jori's brother, gathered in the Koros system. A meeting was held in Teta's palace, where the Empress appointed [[Vontaige]], one of her top [[Advisor/Legends|advisors]] and [[General/Legends|generals]], to help lead her [[Koros fleet|forces]] against the Sith on Koros Major. Although many of the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet's]] [[Warship/Legends|warships]] had been destroyed or damaged in the recent [[Unification Wars (Koros)/Legends|Koros Unification Wars]], Teta believed that they could still make a good accounting of themselves. The citizens of Koros Major were determined to defend the [[Planet/Legends|world]] with their lives and launched their [[Battleship/Legends|battleships]]. The Koros military began to take up defensive positions inside the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Cinnagar/Legends|Cinnagar]], and even Teta herself began preparing for battle, donning a heavy suit of [[battle armor]] and wielding a large [[Lance/Legends|lance]]. Jori Daragon also armed herself with a [[protosaber]] that had been given to her by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]].<ref name="encounter" />

==The battle==
{{Quote|Beautiful Cinnagar must not fall to the Sith!|Empress Teta|<ref name="encounter" />}}
[[File:Aarrba_death.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Massassi warriors kill Aarrba the Hutt.]]
The Sith fleet promptly began to bombard the planet, sending Cinnagar civilians into a panic. Numerous [[Dropship/Legends|dropships]], containing Sith [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warriors and [[Sith war behemoth|war behemoths]], launched toward the planet's surface. The Koros military utilized [[anti-aircraft gun]]s as countermeasures against the incoming [[Starship/Legends|ships]]. These shots were often wild during the onslaught, and some nearly hit residential homes and businesses. Meanwhile, Gav Daragon, despite the objections of his subordinates, took a contingent of Massassi guards with him when he decided to go to the surface of Koros Major in a dropship. Gav told them that he intended to try a shut down of the city's defenses to facilitate a near-[[Blood/Legends|bloodless]] victory. He had an ulterior motive, however; he wanted to know what had become of his sister, Jori. He landed near the [[Aarrba's Repair Dock|repair dock]] of a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] named [[Aarrba]], an old acquaintance of the [[Daragon family]]. When Gav asked Aarrba for his sister's location, however, the Hutt merely spat in his face. Interpreting this as an attack, Gav's Massassi guards stabbed the Hutt in his abdomen. Mortally wounded, the Hutt rolled off his [[Hoversled/Legends|hoversled]] and crushed the Massassi guards to [[Death/Legends|death]].<ref name="encounter" />

Jori Daragon arrived on the scene at that moment and spotted the dead body of Aarrba. Blaming her brother for the Hutt's death, Jori became enraged and slew the lone remaining Massassi guard with her activated [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].<ref name="encounter" /> With tears in her [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], she confronted Gav about Aarrba's death. Overcome with the emotion of the situation, Gav Daragon fled the scene. He returned to his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] and abandoned the battlefield of Koros Major to confront Naga Sadow, hoping to end the war that had ravaged his home and driven him and his sister apart. Without their commander, the Sith force was defeated by Teta and the Koros military.<ref name="dogs" />

==Aftermath==
Jori Daragon later led Teta's fleet in pursuit of her brother Gav, who had found himself in a predicament: he had become trapped aboard [[Naga Sadow's Mothership|Sadow's empty Sith Meditation Sphere]] after Sadow tricked him into coming aboard the [[Sith Meditation Sphere|vessel]] by promising to meet him face-to-face. Gav gave the Koros forces the location of the Sith Empire, but soon afterward, Sadow reappeared and triggered a secret weapon to [[Battle at Primus Goluud|destroy]] the nearby [[Star/Legends|star]] of [[Primus Goluud/Legends|Primus Goluud]]. The [[Supernova/Legends|ensuing explosion]] killed Gav Daragon.<ref name="dogs" /> Sadow returned to the Sith Empire defeated; his forces had taken losses at Koros Major, [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and [[Kirrek]], thereby ending any hope of a victory for the Sith Empire. Teta's forces followed him to the Empire's holdings and defeated him [[Second battle of Korriban|in battle over Korriban]], forcing the Dark Lord of the Sith to fake his death and flee to the faraway [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].<ref name="end">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 5]]</ref> The city of Cinnagar was greatly damaged in the battle, but it had been fully rebuilt by [[3996 BBY]] and was flourishing at that time.<ref name="DLotS">''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The battle on Koros Major is an event that first appeared in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire|''Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire'']], a story arc of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']], written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and released in [[1997]].<ref name="DarkHorse-1">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/95-291/Star-Wars-Tales-of-the-Jedi---The-Fall-of-the-Sith-Empire-1-of-5|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire #1 (of 5)}}</ref><ref name="DarkHorse-2">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/95-305/Star-Wars-Tales-of-the-Jedi---The-Fall-of-the-Sith-Empire-5-of-5|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5}}</ref> The lead-up to the battle and much of the action is featured in the arc's third issue, [[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 3|''First Encounter'']],<ref name="encounter" /> with closure to the Daragons' saga during the battle being given in the fourth issue, [[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 4|''The Dogs of War'']]. In these appearances, the battle is illustrated by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]]<ref name="dogs" />
